subject: Comms relay drops when switching between antenna arrays
description: Every time I switch from the hardline uplink to the RF mesh antenna, the comms relay drops like it's personally offended. Have to re-establish the link manually each time, which involves staring at a progress bar for 45 seconds while the entire Gamma Sector sits in radio silence wondering if something ate the antenna. Started after last Tuesday's station firmware update — you know, the one nobody asked for and nobody was warned about and nobody has been held accountable for. Using SubComm v3.2 on Deck 4. The old version worked fine. I'm just saying.

subject: Mission Planner crashes and Star Chart shows old waypoints
description: Two problems since the terminal swap yesterday: (1) Mission Planner shows a white screen then crashes on launch -- tried rebooting the console twice. (2) Star Chart is displaying old waypoints from 2024 that were purged months ago. Both started right after the terminal swap. Might be related, might not.

subject: Holographic display not working in main briefing chamber
description: The holographic projector in the main briefing chamber won't activate. Tried the remote initializer and the manual switch on the emitter housing. Deep Space Command delegation arriving in ~25 minutes for the final review on a 2.3B-credit colony contract -- our Mission Director is presenting. No backup chamber seats 12. Can someone respond immediately?

subject: RED ALERT -- all systems failing!!!
description: Nothing works. I cant access anything. My console is just spinning. I've been floating here for 20 minutes tapping the screen like it owes me credits. The holographic display flickers and then shows me a loading cursor that I swear is judging me — it made eye contact. A cursor. Made eye contact. Please fix ASAP i have mission deadlines today and my commanding officer is already asking for the deliverable. I tried rebooting but the console just played the startup chime and went right back to spinning. Even the nutrient synthesizer next to my station is giving me attitude — I asked for coffee and it dispensed what I can only describe as warm disappointment with notes of betrayal. I'm starting to think Deck 5 is haunted. Specifically, my workstation is haunted. By the ghost of whoever last used it and clearly didn't log out properly.

subject: Re: [Signal SIG-0847] Scheduled maintenance notification
description:
Thank you for your transmission. I am currently on scheduled shore leave at Titan Station from March 24-28 with limited access to subspace relay (the signal lag from Saturn orbit is approximately 76 minutes each way, so please factor that into your expectations for a timely reply). For urgent matters during my absence, please contact my colleague Alex at alex.wu@cdss.space. For non-urgent matters, I will respond upon my return — assuming the shuttle back doesn't get rerouted again. Last time it took three extra days because someone forgot to file the flight plan and we ended up orbiting Enceladus while the pilot 'recalculated.'

Best regards,
Priya Sharma
Director of Astrogation

P.S. — If the Centauri transit window calculations look wrong while I'm gone, do NOT attempt to correct them manually. Just wait. Trust me on this.

subject: Unauthorized biometric scan attempts on my profile
description: Got 6 biometric scan alert notifications this morning -- someone attempted to authenticate against my crew profile from an unregistered terminal in Sector 7-G. I haven't been in that sector. Already reset my access codes but worried the profile may already be compromised. I have classified mission briefings and tactical operations documents in my secure data vault. Should I be concerned about data exposure?

subject: Nightly telemetry sync failing -- 403 on station data archive
description: Telemetry pipeline 'prod-sensor-ingest' has been failing since 03:00 UTC with 403 Forbidden on the station data archive. The service credential either expired or lost its Data Contributor role during last week's archive cleanup — which was supposed to be 'routine maintenance' and instead became 'routine destruction of everything that relies on those credentials.' Feeds the daily mission briefing report used for the Monday command review — you know, the one where the Commander asks 'why don't we have telemetry data?' and everyone looks at their boots and wishes the floor would open up into the vacuum of space. Pipeline runs in Sector Alpha, data-core-prod. Last successful run Saturday 03:00 UTC. That's 48 hours of telemetry we're not ingesting, for anyone keeping score. The Commander is. She has a spreadsheet.

subject: Low priority -- small database issue
description:
Hey team. The production telemetry database has been returning timeout errors for 2 hours. ~40% of sensor queries to the data banks are failing with 500 errors. Life support monitoring systems are seeing intermittent data gaps — nothing critical yet, but the oxygen recycler dashboard hasn't updated in 45 minutes, which is the kind of sentence that should probably concern me more than it does. Station diagnostics show connection pool exhaustion on the primary data node. Marked as low priority since the backup node is still handling some traffic.

Let me know when you get a chance. No rush. Unless the oxygen thing turns out to be a real thing, in which case: rush.

subject: Re: SIG-2834 -- Airlock badge still broken, Deck 7
description: Following up on SIG-2834 from last week. Biometric badge still doesn't work for the Deck 7 airlock after the access system migration. Was told it'd be fixed by Friday — it's Monday and I still can't cycle through. Have to wait for someone to manually override the door every morning, which means standing in the corridor in my EVA undersuit while the atmospheric processor hums judgmentally above my head and the Deck 7 cats stare at me from the ventilation grate like I'm the one who doesn't belong here. Third time reporting this. At this point I've spent more time filing signals about this airlock than actually passing through it. I'm starting to think the airlock has developed sentience and a personal grudge. If it starts talking to me, I'm filing a threat detection report.

subject: New crew member arriving March 31 -- full setup needed
description:
New senior science officer (Alex Rivera) reporting for duty March 31. Arriving on the 0600 supply shuttle from Earth — assuming the shuttle is on time, which it hasn't been since the Q3 schedule change. Needs:
- Dedicated console terminal (high-spec, 32GB memory core) — the science team's last new arrival got a terminal that predates the station refit and it took 4 minutes to render a molecular model
- Standard flight software + SciAnalytics/SpectraLab suite
- Telemetry workspace access (lab-science-prod)
- Data archive read access
- Deck 3 quarters assignment and lab badge — please not the quarters next to the atmospheric processor, the last person in those quarters transferred off-station citing 'persistent vibrational disturbance' which is a polite way of saying 'the wall shakes all night'
- Added to science-team comm channel and briefing distribution

Reports to me. Please have everything ready by March 28 for orientation walkthrough. Last time we onboarded someone, they arrived to find no biometric profile, no terminal, and a quarters assignment on a deck that was being renovated. Let's not do that again.

subject: Biometric scanner not accepting my iris scan
description: I'm getting an error every time I try to authenticate via the iris scanner outside the Flight Deck. The BioAuth panel shows the scan prompt but when I look into it, it says 'Biometric match failed — recalibration required.' Tried re-registering my iris pattern in the system, same result. My colleague suggested it might be because I just came back from a 6-hour EVA and my eyes are still adjusting to station lighting — apparently staring into the infinite void of space for six hours does things to your retinal signature. Who knew. I can still get through using the backup PIN code but that's slower, and there's always a line of people behind me sighing audibly while I type it in like some kind of pre-spaceflight cave dweller. Yesterday someone actually said 'they let anyone on the Flight Deck these days' and I chose violence internally but diplomacy externally. Not super urgent, just annoying and mildly existentially humiliating.

subject: FYI -- Long-range sensor array display flickering
description:
Display on the long-range sensor array console started flickering around 10am station time. It's not constant — happens for a few seconds every 5-10 minutes, which is exactly often enough to be infuriating but not often enough to be obviously broken. I swapped the data conduit to a different port and it still does it. Sensor data itself seems fine, readings are updating normally. Just the display. The flickering does make it look like the deep-space void is winking at me, which is either a hardware fault or an existential crisis waiting to happen.

Also, unrelated — can you check if my NavStar Pro license is still active? I got an expiry warning last week but I can still log in. Knowing this station's licensing system, it'll let me in right up until I'm in the middle of a critical trajectory calculation and then lock me out mid-equation.

subject: Potential data breach -- classified crew records exposed
description: During a routine security audit — you know, the kind of audit that starts with 'let's just double-check something' and ends with someone saying 'oh no' very quietly — we discovered that a data archive containing classified PII for ~1,200 crew personnel across the Kepler Station was shared with unsecured 'anyone with the link' permissions. 'Anyone with the link' means anyone. In space. Where signals bounce between relay satellites and get cached in places we've never heard of. The archive has been accessible since at least February 15. We've revoked the sharing permissions as of this morning but need to determine: (1) who created the public link (I have theories, none of them flattering), (2) whether unauthorized parties accessed the data, and (3) whether this triggers a notification obligation under Space Authority data protection regulations. If the answer to (3) is yes, I need to know before the Commander does, because she finds out about these things approximately 12 seconds after they happen through means I have not been able to identify. This is a potential regulatory incident. It is also potentially my last day as Data Protection Commander if I don't handle it correctly.

subject: thanks!
description: got it working, appreciate the quick turnaround. the atmospheric recycler on Deck 4 is humming normally again and the corridor no longer smells like burnt ozone and regret. you guys are the best!! 🚀

subject: Slight delay in navigation calculations -- may be nothing
description: Noticing about a 200ms delay in navigation calculations on the Gamma Sector relay this morning. Normally we're at ~50ms — which is already not great, but at least the helm officers don't visibly twitch. Latency seems to spike right around 09:00 SST and hasn't recovered. Not sure if it's on our side or the relay beacon. The helm officers haven't complained yet but that's because they haven't noticed yet, and the moment they do, I will receive approximately 47 signals simultaneously, all marked URGENT, all saying slightly different things, and all CC'ing the Commander. If this gets to 500ms during the Centauri transit window, we'll have a real problem — specifically the kind of problem where a starship misses its insertion burn by 0.003 degrees and ends up in a progressively deteriorating orbit around nothing in particular. Just flagging early. You're welcome.

subject: FlightOS won't load after credential rotation
description: Rotated my station credentials this morning per the quarterly rotation protocol. Now FlightOS gives me 'SSO token invalid -- contact your system administrator.' Tried logging out and back in to the terminal, same issue. Other applications (CommLink, Star Chart, DataVault) work fine. I need FlightOS to process end-of-cycle trajectory calculations -- close of business today is the deadline.

subject: Emergency beacon -- crew member having issues with something
description:
[Transcribed from emergency beacon voice transmission, 15:42 UTC]

Caller: Hi yeah so um my thing... the thing on my console... it keeps doing the... you know when you tap the holographic interface and it goes to the... [garbled] ...and then nothing happens? I've been trying for like two days. My colleague Janet said to hail Mission Control. I think it might be the... [garbled] ...something about permissions? I don't know. Can someone just come look at it? I'm on Deck 5, near the galley. The one that smells like reconstituted pasta and broken dreams. My name's Bob. No wait, Robert. Robert Chen. Not the Robert Chen in Astrogation, the other one. Not the other other one either — there are three of us. I'm the one who doesn't know what's wrong with his console. Which, now that I say it out loud, doesn't narrow it down at all.

subject: Commander Martinez can't project holo-display in command briefing -- happening now
description: Commander Martinez (Mission Commander, Deep Space Operations) is in the command briefing right now and can't project his holographic display. The briefing is with the admiralty and allied fleet commanders. He's tried projecting from both his personal terminal and the chamber's integrated holo-emitter. Other participants can project fine. His terminal shows 'Display sharing blocked by station policy.' Briefing has been going on for 10 minutes without his presentation. He's requesting someone report to the command deck briefing chamber immediately.

subject: Crew member departure -- access revocation needed, last day March 21
description:
Jessica Palmer (jessica.palmer@cdss.space) in the Tactical Operations division has received transfer orders to the Kepler Station outpost. Her last duty day aboard CDSS is Friday March 21 — she's on the 1800 shuttle. Please:
- Disable her BioScan ID at 18:00 EST on March 21 (not before — she needs airlock access to get to the shuttle bay, and locking her out early would be both awkward and logistically complicated)
- Revoke airlock access and all remote terminal privileges
- Wipe her assigned console terminal (StarTerm X-40, asset tag SS-T-4892)
- Transfer ownership of her data vault files to her supervisor David Liu (david.liu@cdss.space)
- Remove her from all comm channels and briefing distribution lists
- Archive her message logs per our 7-year retention protocol

Note: Jessica has access to classified investigation files related to the Sector 7-G incident. Please ensure these are preserved but not accessible after her departure. Last thing we need is classified tactical data floating around on an unattended terminal.

subject: Fabricator on Deck 3 not working
description:
The main fabricator near the mess hall on Deck 3 stopped producing. Jobs queue up in the system but nothing materializes in the output bay — the fabricator just sits there humming contentedly like it's achieved enlightenment and transcended the need to fabricate things. I tried submitting from my personal terminal and from the shared console next to it — same result. The fabricator display shows 'Ready' and feedstock hoppers are full, which is the fabricator equivalent of a coworker saying "I'm fine" while their desk is on fire.

Other crew on the deck are having the same issue. We need it for replacement hull gaskets going out for a scheduled repair on the observation deck viewport — the current gaskets are rated for another 72 hours before they drop below spec. Not an emergency yet, but 'not an emergency yet' is how every emergency on this station started. It's practically our motto. Someone should embroider it on a pillow. A pressurized pillow.

subject: How do I reserve a briefing chamber?
description:
Hi, I just came aboard the station last week from the Earth rotation and I need to reserve the large briefing chamber on Deck 4 for a crew social on Friday. I checked the station scheduling system but I can't figure out how to book it — every time I click 'Reserve' it asks for a Mission Justification Code and I don't have one because this is a social event, not a mission. Do I need a code for pizza night? Is pizza night a mission? (Honestly, for morale purposes at 0.3 AU from the nearest pizza restaurant, it absolutely should be classified as mission-critical.)

Also, do I need to order catering through Mission Control or is that a different department? The nutrient synthesizer can technically make pizza but the results are... aspirational at best. It produces something flat and warm and vaguely circular, which I suppose is technically the minimum viable definition of pizza. Someone told me there's an actual kitchen on Deck 6 but I haven't found it yet. I also haven't found Deck 6. This station is not well-signed for new arrivals. I've been on three decks that claimed to be Deck 4 and none of them were the same deck. Thanks!

subject: Comms encryption cert expiring on relay-gateway.cdss.space -- 3 days left
description: Station monitoring flagged that the encryption certificate on relay-gateway.cdss.space expires on March 20. This is the primary relay gateway that all external comms and allied fleet integrations route through. If the cert expires, every external communication link will start failing with encryption handshake errors. The cert was originally provisioned manually by an engineer who transferred off-station 6 months ago. I don't know if auto-renewal was ever configured. We need to renew or replace it before Thursday.

subject: I think my terminal has malware
description: My terminal has been super slow for two days. Programs take forever to load, the cooling fan runs constantly, and I got a couple of weird pop-ups that said something about 'updating system components.' I think I might have picked up malware from that suspicious transmission I accidentally opened last week. I ran the onboard security scanner and it didn't find anything but I'm still worried. Should I stop using the terminal until someone checks it?

subject: Fwd: You have won 5,000,000 credits -- claim now!!!
description:
---------- Forwarded transmission ----------
From: Galactic Prize Authority <claims@totallylegit-prizes.ng>
Date: March 16, 2026
Subject: You have won 5,000,000 credits -- claim now!!!

Dear Valued Crew Member,

Congratulations! Your subspace relay address has been selected as the winner of our annual Galactic Lottery. You have won the sum of 5,000,000 credits. This is definitely real and not at all suspicious.

To claim your prize, please access the link below and enter your full name, crew ID, and banking credentials:
https://claim-your-credits.totallylegit-prizes.ng

--- end forwarded transmission ---

Hey Mission Control -- got this transmission from deep space, is this legit? Should I access the link? Looks suspicious but I don't want to miss out if it's real. Also 5 million credits would really help with my transfer request to a station that has real coffee instead of whatever the nutrient synthesizer thinks coffee is. Asking for a friend. The friend is me.

subject: Station compute node 'prod-analytics-01' unreachable since reboot
description: Compute node prod-analytics-01 (High-Performance E16 class, Sector Alpha, analytics-core-prod) became unreachable after a scheduled reboot at 05:00 UTC for OS patching. Remote shell and diagnostic ports both timeout. Station system manager shows node status as 'Running' but boot diagnostics show it stuck at 'Applying configuration.' Signal barrier rules haven't changed. This node powers the command analytics dashboard that the senior staff uses for the morning mission briefing at 9 AM EST. We have about 2 hours.
